seo.primaryKeyword: what score do you need to get a 5 on AP Psych
seo.title: What Score Do You Need to Get a 5 on AP Psych?
seo.description: Estimate what MCQ and FRQ scores you may need to get a 5 on AP Psychology. Use the AP Psych Score Calculator to test different score scenarios.
seo.h1: What Score Do You Need to Get a 5 on AP Psych?

content.directAnswer: A rough target for a 5 is about 75% or higher on the estimated composite score, but the safest answer depends on your MCQ and FRQ combination.

sections.heading: Estimated Target for a 5
sections.body: Aim for a strong combined performance across both multiple-choice and free-response sections. Use the calculator to test different combinations.

sections.heading: Can a Strong FRQ Help?
sections.body: Yes. A stronger FRQ score can help offset some missed MCQ questions, but both sections matter.

faqs.question: Is this calculator official?
faqs.answer: No. This calculator is an independent estimate and is not affiliated with or endorsed by College Board.

faqs.question: Can actual AP score cutoffs vary?
faqs.answer: Yes. Actual score thresholds can vary by year and exam form, so this tool should be used for planning only.

faqs.question: Does FRQ performance matter for AP Psych?
faqs.answer: Yes. FRQ performance contributes to the composite estimate, so a stronger FRQ score can offset some missed multiple-choice questions.

faqs.question: Should I use this before or after the exam?
faqs.answer: You can use it before the exam for target planning or after the exam to estimate possible score outcomes.
